Cast assignments:
1. Jerry Gold — Narrator/graduate (Deadpan News Host persona). Dry delivery of the double-major claim. The pun lands because he treats it as a completely serious life decision.
2. Milo Quip — Skeptical listener (Snarky Student persona). Reaction shot. His face says "I can't believe you just said that" which validates the audience's groan.

---

## Concept A — "The Graduation Speech"
Style: College graduation ceremony, warm wholesome energy undercut by the groan pun
Duration: 8.6s — Tight two-beat joke, no fat needed.

Master video prompt:
"Outdoor college graduation ceremony, cartoon illustration 2.5D style, clean bold lines, vertical 9:16. Jerry Gold (middle-aged dad, brown hair, handlebar mustache) wearing a black graduation cap and gown over his teal jacket, standing at a podium with a microphone. Behind him: a university banner, folding chairs, sunny day. Milo Quip (teen boy, spiky brown hair, dark t-shirt) seated in the audience, arms crossed, unimpressed. Caption-safe lower 20%."

Scene 1 (0.0–2.0s): HOOK — The Graduate
An outdoor college campus quad on a sunny day. Green lawn, trees, a small portable stage with a wooden podium and microphone. Jerry Gold stands at the podium — a middle-aged man with tousled brown hair, a handlebar mustache, wearing a black graduation cap and gown. The gown is slightly too small and his teal jacket collar pokes out from underneath. He grips the podium edges with both hands, looking out at the audience with complete sincerity. Behind him, a banner reads "CONGRATULATIONS GRADUATES" in blocky university letters. Warm golden afternoon light. He is about to say something he considers very important.

Scene 2 (2.0–5.0s): SETUP — The Claim
Same graduation stage and podium. Jerry Gold (brown hair, mustache, graduation cap and gown over teal jacket) leans slightly into the microphone. His expression is earnest — proud, even. One hand has lifted off the podium in a small explanatory gesture. In the foreground audience, Milo Quip is visible — a teenage boy with spiky brown hair and a dark charcoal t-shirt, slumped in a folding chair with his arms crossed. Milo's expression is neutral-to-skeptical, one eyebrow slightly raised. Other blurred audience members fill chairs on either side. Sunny outdoor lighting.
Caption: "I double majored in Geology and Piano."

Scene 3 (5.0–5.4s): THE PAUSE
Same stage. Jerry Gold at the podium, graduation cap slightly tilted. He pauses. His mouth is closed. He looks out at the audience with the expression of a man about to deliver wisdom. The microphone gleams in front of him. Milo Quip in the foreground audience has uncrossed his arms — sensing something coming. Anticipation.

Scene 4 (5.4–8.6s): PUNCHLINE — The Landing
Same stage. Jerry Gold at the podium leans into the microphone with a slight nod, as if confirming a life philosophy. His expression has not changed — still completely sincere. In the foreground, Milo Quip's face has collapsed into visible disgust — eyes half-closed, mouth slightly open in a silent groan, one hand coming up to cover his face. The contrast between Jerry's pride and Milo's suffering IS the joke. Golden afternoon light. A few blurred audience members around Milo look similarly pained.
Caption: "I've always had a passion for rock music."

Caption plan: 0.0–2.0s no text | 2.0–5.0s setup | 5.4–8.6s punchline
Voice: Jerry earnest and sincere throughout. Slight pause 0.3s. Punchline delivered as genuine life reflection. Milo makes no sound.
Risk: LOW. Clean joke. Fallback: simplify to Jerry at a desk/table talking to camera (removes graduation set complexity).
Jerry hook: "Clean, visual, Milo's reaction shot does half the work. Easy greenlight."
Vince: 9:16, 8.6s, 4 scenes, mustKeep=[Scene 4 punchline+Milo reaction, Scene 2 setup], trims=[Scene 3 pause can shorten to 0.2s]

## Concept B — "The Career Counselor"
Style: Office meeting, career counselor reviews Jerry's transcript with increasing confusion
Duration: 9.0s — Slightly longer for the two-person dynamic.

Master video prompt:
"Small career counselor office, cartoon illustration 2.5D style, clean bold lines, vertical 9:16. A cluttered desk with papers, a computer monitor, a small desk plant. Jerry Gold (brown hair, mustache, teal jacket) seated across from Zoe Sparks (orange-magenta two-tone hair, teal glasses, teal jacket with star pin) who sits behind the desk holding a folder. Office fluorescent lighting. Caption-safe lower 20%."

Scene 1 (0.0–2.2s): HOOK — The Office
A small career counselor's office with pale walls and fluorescent overhead lighting. A cluttered wooden desk sits in the center with stacked folders, a desktop computer monitor, a small potted succulent, and a nameplate. Behind the desk sits Zoe Sparks — a young woman with short choppy orange-and-magenta two-tone hair, round teal glasses, teal jacket with a star pin, and hoop earrings. She holds an open folder and reads it with a polite but puzzled expression. Across the desk, Jerry Gold sits in a visitor's chair — a middle-aged man with brown hair, handlebar mustache, and teal jacket over a golden-yellow hoodie. He looks calm and confident, hands folded in his lap. He sees nothing unusual about why he's here.

Scene 2 (2.2–5.2s): SETUP — The Transcript
Same office. Zoe Sparks behind the desk (orange-magenta hair, teal glasses, folder in hand) looks up from the folder at Jerry Gold across the desk (brown hair, mustache, teal jacket, relaxed in visitor chair). She's reading from his academic transcript, her expression a mix of professional courtesy and genuine confusion — brow slightly furrowed, one finger pointing at something on the page. Jerry sits with quiet pride, chin slightly raised. On the desk between them, the open folder is visible with lines of text (unreadable). The desktop monitor shows a generic spreadsheet. Fluorescent lighting.
Caption: "I double majored in Geology and Piano."

Scene 3 (5.2–5.6s): THE BEAT
Same office. Zoe Sparks (teal glasses, orange-magenta hair) has lowered the folder to the desk. She stares at Jerry Gold across the desk. Her mouth is slightly open. She is processing. Jerry's expression hasn't changed — still proud, still patient. The silence between them is the comedy. Fluorescent buzz.

Scene 4 (5.6–9.0s): PUNCHLINE — The Explanation
Same office. Jerry Gold (brown hair, mustache, teal jacket) leans forward slightly in the visitor's chair, one hand raised in a small explanatory gesture, delivering his reasoning as if it's the most logical thing in the world. His expression is warm, sincere. Across the desk, Zoe Sparks (teal glasses, orange-magenta hair) has closed the folder and set it down. Her eyes have gone wide behind her glasses. Her mouth is a thin line. She is trying very hard to remain professional. The potted succulent on the desk sits between them, indifferent.
Caption: "I've always had a passion for rock music."

Caption plan: 0.0–2.2s no text | 2.2–5.2s setup | 5.6–9.0s punchline
Voice: Jerry sincere, conversational, explaining a life choice. Zoe silent (reaction only). 0.3s pause. Punchline delivered like it explains everything.
Risk: LOW. Fallback: single-shot Jerry talking to camera if two-person office scene fails.
Jerry hook: "Zoe's slow-dawning horror sells it. The office setting makes the pun feel like a real life decision someone made, which makes it funnier."
Vince: 9:16, 9.0s, 4 scenes, mustKeep=[Scene 4 punchline+Zoe reaction, Scene 2 transcript reveal], trims=[Scene 3 can cut to 0.2s]
